content: We can reframe working memory retention in long-horizon active learning as a soft constraint in a maximum entropy RL objective, where sustained neural synchrony between prefrontal and posterior cortices maintains an optimal entropy budget that balances exploration of new information and consolidation of retained state representations. This framework implies that working memory does not simply store activations, but actively modulates the entropy of the agent's state distribution to avoid premature forgetting over long horizons.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
